title: Light and Buttery Egyptian Rice Koshari
description: A simple, comforting Egyptian dish made with rice, lentils, and a blend of aromatic spices.
instructions: Rinse the lentils in a fine mesh sieve until the water runs clear.
In a 3-quart ttukbaegi earthenware pot, combine the water, lentils, cumin, coriander, allspice, and salt.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
While the lentils cook, heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the rice to the saucepan and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes.
Add 4 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the water has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Fluff the rice with a fork and stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
To serve, place a scoop of the rice mixture on a plate, followed by a spoonful of the cooked lentils.
